judgment.summary: Apollo Global Management shows a significant disconnect between its trailing and forward P/E, suggesting strong future growth expectations are already priced in, while current revenue is declining.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported quarter (03-31-2026) EPS of $1.94 beat the estimate of $1.88 by 2.9%. Next earnings date is 2026-08-04.
positives: Forward P/E of 12.44x is significantly lower than trailing P/E of 83.17x, indicating strong future earnings growth expectations.
PEG ratio of 0.70 suggests the stock may be undervalued relative to its expected earnings growth.
Current ratio of 2.02 and quick ratio of 1.98 indicate strong short-term liquidity.
Analyst consensus is a buy with a mean target price of $149.65, suggesting upside potential.
The company beat its last reported EPS estimate, showing positive earnings performance.
negatives: Revenue growth was negative at -9.2% year-over-year.
Profit margin of 3.7% is relatively low.
Return on equity of 8.5% and return on assets of 0.7% are modest.
Debt/Equity ratio of 101.27% is high, indicating significant leverage.
Dividend payout ratio of 128.3% is unsustainable, suggesting potential dividend cuts.

news.paragraph: Apollo Global Management (APO) is facing a mixed news cycle as it expands into sustainable waste management, a move that Simply Wall St. suggests may warrant caution regarding its valuation. Concurrently, the company is reportedly being urged by teacher groups to probe the CEO's work with Donald Trump, introducing a potential governance concern. These headlines do not point to an imminent catalyst but suggest an environment where traders should consider both strategic expansion and potential reputational or valuation headwinds.

headlines.title: Apollo Expands Into Sustainable Waste Management While Valuation Signals Caution
